page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, running on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% with a score of 1105.49, while the multi-core CPU performance reaches an average tier at 8682.55. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show poor performance, with gzip compression scoring in the bottom 10% at 18.97 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 7.17 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the average tier, measuring 640.61 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 61.2 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable resource density, though the older processor architecture limits single-threaded efficiency. Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput both rank in the weak category, scoring 2186.6 and 5.98 GB/sec respectively. However, the instance achieves an average rating in Redis SET operations, processing 10.22 million operations per second. This profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, and basic caching layers that do not require high single-core throughput or local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between average multi-core capabilities and weak single-core execution. The stress-ng multi-core test and Redis SET operations (10.22 million operations/sec) achieve average ratings, whereas single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with uncached read speeds of 61.2 GB/sec. Given its dedicated CPU allocation but lower single-core efficiency, this instance is suited for balanced, multi-threaded workloads, basic caching, and general-purpose applications that do not demand high-speed local storage or intensive single-threaded processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.xlarge is an 8-vCPU general-purpose instance with 32 GB RAM and an Intel Xeon processor, offering average multi-core performance and weak single-core execution.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation and stable multi-core performance for non-storage-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average multi-core CPU performance and Redis SET operations throughput
Weak single-core execution and poor single-threaded compression speeds
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Suited for multi-threaded general-purpose applications and caching layers
